svg.text: • 2-SAT solving: implication graph SCCs determine satisfiability + assignment.
• Compiler analysis: mutually recursive functions, SCC of call graph for inlining order.
• Dataflow: dominator analysis, dead-code elimination after collapse-to-DAG.
• Module loading / linking: circular import detection and ordering.
• Deadlock detection: SCC of resource-allocation graph identifies cycle.
• Citation networks / web graph: mutually referencing groups, web "core."
• Static analysis: recursion-set computation, fixed-point iteration grouping.
For each clause (a ∨ b), add edges (¬a → b) and (¬b → a) to implication graph.
Run Tarjan's SCC. Formula is satisfiable iff no variable x and ¬x are in the same SCC.
Assignment: in reverse topological order of condensation, assign each variable so that
x = true if SCC(x) appears before SCC(¬x) in reverse topo order.
Total: O(V + E). Linear-time 2-SAT — among the fastest non-trivial logical algorithms.
2-SAT shows up in scheduling, layout, and constraint problems — Tarjan makes it linear.
